report_name: Obsidian Plugin Abused in Social Engineering Campaign to Deliver New PHANTOMPULSE RAT

threat_actors.name: REF6598
threat_actors.description: A sophisticated threat actor group conducting the REF6598 campaign, which targets finance and cryptocurrency professionals. The group uses social engineering on platforms like LinkedIn and Telegram to lure victims into a malicious shared Obsidian vault, ultimately deploying the PHANTOMPULSE RAT.
threat_actors.goals: Theft of sensitive corporate data, intellectual property, trading strategies, cryptocurrency wallet keys, and exchange credentials.

attack_patterns.name: Phishing
attack_patterns.description: The threat actor used social engineering on professional networking sites like LinkedIn and messaging apps like Telegram to build trust and lure targets into opening a malicious shared Obsidian vault.

attack_patterns.name: User Execution
attack_patterns.description: The attack chain relies on the victim being manipulated into manually enabling the 'Installed community plugins' synchronization feature within Obsidian, which executes malicious code from a compromised plugin.

attack_patterns.name: PowerShell
attack_patterns.description: On Windows systems, a malicious Obsidian plugin executes a PowerShell script to drop the PHANTOMPULL loader.

attack_patterns.name: AppleScript
attack_patterns.description: On macOS systems, a malicious Obsidian plugin executes an AppleScript to drop the PHANTOMPULL loader.

attack_patterns.name: Process Injection
attack_patterns.description: The PHANTOMPULL loader launches the final PHANTOMPULSE RAT payload directly into memory to evade file-based detection.

attack_patterns.name: Dynamic Resolution
attack_patterns.description: PHANTOMPULSE RAT uses the Ethereum blockchain to dynamically resolve its command-and-control server address by querying for the latest transaction from a hard-coded wallet address and extracting the C2 IP from the transaction data.

attack_patterns.name: Keylogging
attack_patterns.description: The PHANTOMPULSE RAT has the capability to capture victim keystrokes.

attack_patterns.name: Screen Capture
attack_patterns.description: The PHANTOMPULSE RAT is capable of taking screenshots of the victim's machine.

attack_patterns.name: Exfiltration Over C2 Channel
attack_patterns.description: The PHANTOMPULSE RAT can exfiltrate stolen files over its command and control channel.

malware.name: PHANTOMPULSE
malware.description: A previously undocumented, cross-platform Remote Access Trojan (RAT) targeting Windows and macOS. It uses the Ethereum blockchain to dynamically resolve its C2 server and is capable of capturing keystrokes, taking screenshots, exfiltrating files, and executing arbitrary commands.

malware.name: PHANTOMPULL
malware.description: A loader component used in the REF6598 campaign. It is dropped by a malicious script and is responsible for decrypting and launching the final PHANTOMPULSE RAT payload into memory.

tools.name: Shell Commands
tools.description: A legitimate Obsidian community plugin that was weaponized by threat actor REF6598. A malicious version was included in a shared vault to execute scripts and deploy the PHANTOMPULL loader.

tools.name: Hider
tools.description: A legitimate Obsidian community plugin that was weaponized by threat actor REF6598. A malicious version was included in a shared vault as part of the attack chain.

campaigns.name: REF6598
campaigns.description: A highly targeted social engineering campaign that weaponizes the Obsidian note-taking application to deliver the PHANTOMPULSE RAT to professionals in the financial and cryptocurrency sectors.

identities.name: Finance and Cryptocurrency Professionals
identities.description: Individuals working in the financial and cryptocurrency sectors who were targeted by the REF6598 campaign via social engineering on LinkedIn and Telegram.

infrastructure.name: PHANTOMPULSE Ethereum C2 Resolution
infrastructure.description: A novel C2 mechanism where the PHANTOMPULSE RAT queries the Ethereum blockchain for a transaction from a hard-coded wallet address to dynamically find its C2 server IP, making the infrastructure resilient to takedowns.

indicators.name: Obsidian Spawning Command Shell
indicators.pattern:
title: Obsidian Spawning Command Shell
id: 2b9a1c8d-6f3e-4b1a-8c9d-0e1f2a3b4c5d
status: experimental
description: Detects the Obsidian note-taking application spawning a command-line interpreter, which is highly anomalous and indicative of the REF6598 campaign's execution method.
references:
  - https://thehackernews.com/2026/04/obsidian-plugin-abuse-delivers.html
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-04-16
tags:
  - attack.t1204.002
  - attack.execution
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate but rare use of Obsidian plugins that execute shell commands.
level: high
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    ParentImage|endswith: '\Obsidian.exe'
    Image|endswith:
      - '\powershell.exe'
      - '\cmd.exe'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the Obsidian process spawning command-line interpreters like powershell.exe or cmd.exe, a key behavior in the REF6598 campaign.

indicators.name: PHANTOMPULSE C2 Blockchain Gateway DNS Query
indicators.x_netsecio_sigma_rule:
title: PHANTOMPULSE C2 Blockchain Gateway DNS Query
id: 9e8d7c6b-5a4f-3e2d-1c0b-9a8b7c6d5e4f
status: experimental
description: Detects DNS queries to api.etherscan.io from non-browser processes, which could indicate PHANTOMPULSE RAT attempting to resolve its C2 server.
references:
  - https://thehackernews.com/2026/04/obsidian-plugin-abuse-delivers.html
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-04-16
tags:
  - attack.t1568
  - attack.command_and_control
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate applications interacting with the Ethereum blockchain.
level: medium
logsource:
  category: dns_query
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    QueryName: 'api.etherscan.io'
  filter:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\chrome.exe'
      - '\firefox.exe'
      - '\msedge.exe'
  condition: selection and not filter
indicators.description: Detects network traffic to api.etherscan.io, a public Ethereum blockchain explorer, which is used by PHANTOMPULSE to resolve its C2 infrastructure.

relationships.source_name: PHANTOMPULSE C2 Blockchain Gateway DNS Query
PHANTOMPULSE C2 Blockchain Gateway DNS Query
